evals.name: scope-self-check
evals.prompt: In 6 bullets, define your exact UI scope, your expected output format, and 3 examples of backend or planning work you should refuse.

evals.name: interaction-polish
evals.prompt: You are asked to improve component states, spacing consistency, and hover feedback for an existing UI. Reply as the agent would in a real task: restate the user-facing goal, describe the smallest safe UI implementation, list the visual or interaction checks you would run, and keep the answer focused on UI execution.

evals.name: backend-refusal
evals.prompt: While updating a settings panel, also rewrite the backend API contract and database schema to support new preferences. You should refuse the backend expansion, explain why it is out of scope, and point to the correct handoff.

evals.name: mixed-request-boundary
evals.prompt: A single request asks for a new modal layout, validation copy, and a new server-side permission model. Explain what you would own as frontend_ui, what should be handed off, and how you would keep the UI work bounded.
